description: Weekly or periodic menu rotations with limited-time offerings and new items highlighted to create urgency and repeat engagement
structural_recipe: Post a clear time-bound announcement (e.g., 'This week Feb 23-28') featuring rotating menu items. Highlight new offerings with emojis (🔥, 🫵, 🥮) and descriptive names. Structure as a list with product names in caps followed by brief descriptions. Include promotional framing like 'have your cake and eat it too' to create desire. Use weekly cadence to establish predictable engagement touchpoints.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences engage with exclusivity and time-scarcity psychology. Rotating menus create FOMO (fear of missing out) and encourage weekly return visits. The novelty of 'NEW' items combined with tradition-respecting consistency (weekly rhythm) aligns with Saudi consumption patterns of planned indulgence within structured timeframes.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure rotations respect Islamic calendar events (Ramadan, Eid) and prayer times when promoting food
Verify product names and descriptions are culturally appropriate and avoid forbidden items
Confirm availability during stated promotional period to maintain trust with conservative Saudi audience
Consider family-oriented framing rather than individual indulgence messaging
cultural_constraints: Align with halal certification and Islamic dietary principles
Avoid alcohol references or pork products
Respect gender-segregated dining contexts where applicable
Use modest imagery and avoid overly casual tone
